intro: Psephellus incanescens, also known as the silvery psammophily, is a flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). Traditionally, Psephellus incanescens is used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial effects, making it beneficial for wound healing and immune system support. The bioactive constituents of this plant include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
